Nobody realustically expects say, New Zealand First to ever get the PM slot. But parties do, in some degree have to curry favour.

replies.author: @Kyrid
replies.text: ​@hellacoorinna9995we have the supreme Court which can overrule any unconstitutional laws presented by state legislation. Everyone in the government has to swear an oath to the constitution, not people. The constitution is the most important document here in America because nothing can override it's wording unless Congress votes on appealing any amendment. That's why the first 2 amendments are the most important. 1. Freedom of speech and 2. The right to bear arms

replies.author: @JohnCottier
replies.text: @hellacoorinna9995 That's where state legislators come in. Those are elected by the people in districts within the state and only pass laws effective over the state. Sometimes they do pass laws that are contrary to the constitution, and when challenged in court, they get overturned. New York and California have conservative state legislators, but the sheer overwhelming number of progressive legislators elected by the democrat voting majority of the state prevent them from having much of a voice, but their voters do have a voice through them. The problem is hyper partisanship of most legislators nowadays, rather than actual care for the country, state, constitution, and constituency.
